content:
- INITIAL ANALYSIS AND KEY LEVELS
Looking at the Dollar Index daily chart, the outlined levels from the first week of January are still relevant. If
we trade through this level, we would attack the sell side liquidity below here, which looks to be in the
crosshairs going forward. As long as we remain below the volume imbalance, all the discount arrays,
relative equal lows, fair value gap, and the order block would be reached. We have observed this small gap
in here between the candle's high and this candle's low. We'll annotate that because we already have the
fair value gap here.

- LIQUIDITY DRAW AND SUPPORT CONDITIONS
Be mindful of Friday's High. The only thing that would change the near-term tune for me, preventing a move
to the 101.30 or 101.29 next draw on liquidity, is if we trade above this fair value gap not the one that's
here, not this one, but the one below Wednesday's low and Friday's high net separation. If we trade above
that, come back down, and find some support, you would have two areas of imbalance. This one and this
one. If we trade down to it like that, that might cause it to find some support and work higher. It looks to me
like it is still heavy, but we have not opened up for the new week for trading for Sunday. So it's about a little
after one o'clock my local time on the East Coast of the United States.

- SHORT-TERM OUTLOOK AND SUNDAY OPENING
We'll look at the hourly chart. You see how we dropped down here, came back up, went back down. This to
me is a balanced price range, so it's more likely in my opinion to gravitate towards this low than that of
going higher. But that can all change on Sunday's opening. It's always, you know, who knows where they're
gonna happen or not. But based on what I'm seeing here on the chart before Sunday's opening on the 15th
of January 2023, I think that it's more likely to remain heavy. I'd like to see it gap down, come back up, fill
the gap, and work towards this level here.

section_title: DXY POTENTIAL REVERSAL AND BEARISH BREAKER
content:
But what I want and what the market gives me is two different things, right? So if we do reverse, and say
we get above the imbalance here and the one that I showed on the daily chart, we do have relative equal
highs up here for buy side liquidity. And then we have the bearish breaker here. So this down-closed
candle, have that annotated on your chart, draw that out in time. For now, I don't think we need to worry
about that. Going ahead, I'm still thinking risk on scenario, which is slightly weaker on the dollar.

- BUY SIDE OBJECTIVES AND IMBALANCE
Alright, Euro Dollar daily chart. Here's the levels we had outlined here and here, and the volume imbalance.
So we worked through, took the buy side, and finally pierced the buy side liquidity here. That's the next
area of buy side liquidity and the sell side imbalance buy side inefficiency, which is a fair value gap here. If
we continuously move higher, pay attention to that in here. Okay, it's too clean, small little imbalanced sell
side's been offered. So if we go higher, we could see it try to reprice into this imbalance there.

- LIQUIDITY AND RETESTING CONDITIONS
Hourly, we have buy side liquidity here. Again, similar vein with what I mentioned on the Dollar Index, we
went up, back down, back and forth, came back down and showed initial strength. Now we do go up into
this imbalance here, this is a fair value gap in the form of a SIBI (sell side imbalance buy side inefficiency).
If it trades above it, then this right here should not come back to be retested until these are taken out, and
then at least between here and here, halfway of that, that would be enough. So about 109, 109.05 for Euro
Dollar that would be my next objective again, barring any kind of reversals here. If we gap down and fill in
and start to trade off, we have sell side resting below here. Sundays are always a 50/50, and I just like to
see the things I would like to see stay intact on the higher time frame, which would be reaching higher for
Euro and lower on Dollar.

- EXPANSION AND CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT
E-mini S&P, this is our weekly chart. So we had a nice expansion last week, and I'm drawing your attention
to half of this wick, so this is consequent encroachment on a wick, basically halfway point. And be mindful
of this level here. Okay, so I don't have the annotation because it's been too many lines in here. On the
downside, let's just say we drop back down and we drift lower, this low of this candle here, which is the high
of the fair value gap represented by these three candles, it would need a support price here or half of a
consequent encroachment. I would not want to see it trade back down in. So the last line of defense for
Bulls in my opinion longer term would be midpoint of this fair value gap on the weekly chart. Reaching for
an objective of the midpoint of this weekly candle, so this candle here several weeks ago, we're measuring
that wick in half of it. The way you get that level, drop your Fibonacci on this open to High where the 50
level is, note that on your chart.

- ORDER BLOCKS AND GAP SCENARIOS
Hourly chart on E-mini S&P, bullish order block. This is the levels I gave you on Twitter. Okay, so I gave you
the order block in here and relative equal highs, and we were looking for it to try to trade down into this
area, maybe take out the low, which went way back down into this wick. And we've taken buy side, so I
want to see it try to expand up into 4030, that was one of the objectives. We got the 4020, which is fine, but
didn't get 4030. So I'd like to see a gap up there, gap up, trade back down, close the gap and then continue
on, that would be the best thing for me technically to expect. But if we gap lower, fill in and start to sell off a
little bit, that would be a little bit concerning for me. It's a small little gap in here, if we do get a gap lower
you might want to come back and work inside that level there, but it would be better to leave that open, just
like this one was. Okay, so that could be a measuring gap. So Breakaway Gap, measuring Gap sets the
stage for around 4045, 4040.

- LIQUIDITY AND DISCOUNT ARRAY
Buy side liquidity wrestling above relative equal highs. Here's all of the business, he wants me to call all the
CPI out live on a one minute chart all morning long. And then on Friday we had the market drop lower.
Here's your low wick, there, split that in half, that's consequent encroachment, which is also half of the fair
value gap. Go back through the video and remember where these levels are and where they're anchored
to. Half of that point of this to here and half of this wick, that is a discount array.

- FAIR VALUE GAPS AND ORDER BLOCKS
Market trades back up, back into a fair value gap, then there's how we rolled up, overlapped, rolled up,
overlap back to the previous key impulse high here, rolled up and then left this small little gap here which it
came back later on, overlapped there and rolled right back up. Small little gap came back in, back to the
order block, rallied up, back down in, rolled it up.

section_title: BULLISH BREAKER AND MARKET STRUCTURE SHIFT
content:
Breaker above it, filling support rallies. Notice it's not just going above the high here, draw a line from that
like support resistance teaches you, it digs in. What is it digging into? This actual candle right in here is that
your bullish breaker and it's finally gravitating towards the buy side liquidity at 4020 as I mentioned last
week. On a one-minute chart on E-mini S&P, here's that breaker drawn out. The market trades down into
the fair value gap and to consequent encroachment of that candle's wick as I mentioned before, dropping
down. Go back through the video a couple times and you'll see what this is because I gave you homework,
I'm actually spelling it all out for you here. Short term high, trade to the consequent encroachment twice
and then breaks higher. This candle here breaks above the short-term high, so there's a shift in Market
structure with all the reasons why you would expect it to potentially provide that and it left a fair value gap
here. Trades that into it, beautiful redelivery order block.

Rallies all over the back and forth balancing price action as it goes higher. Buy side here, back into a fair
value gap there once more. This is reclaimed, in other words, it's going back into an area or cutting through
candles because we're not supply and demand. We rally up, order block finds support, trades through.
Look at the candle punching right through it one pass above their own breaker, back down in support
consolidation, rallies, digs into the buy side liquidity at the 4020 level and above the old High.

section_title: FIVE MINUTE MARKET MAKER BUY MODEL
content:
And ultimately on a five minute chart you can see this is the market maker buy model. Consolidation, leave
the consolidation, come back in, this is your sell distribution. Smart money reversal, low risk buy imbalance,
carry it through, cut through candles, trace back down in accumulation of longs, rallies, whatever you got.
One more buy, secondary buy consequent encroachment of the gap midpoint, rallies, order block, find
some support, rallies consolidation and then runs it right up into near the close of the day on Friday to get
to the 4020 level and clearing the old high for buy side liquidity.

That's what we're looking at going ahead for the week. I'm continuously looking for reasons to allow
bullishness on my buy side for index Futures but being aware that obviously we can gap opening on the
week and change everything. So we don't trade on Sundays. We prefer to see what the opening is going to
be at 9:30 on Mondays, so that way the traders can digest and build their sentiment ideas, build their
liquidity pools based on their positions ahead of 9:30 opening and then we'll see who they punish next.
Hope you found this insightful, until next time be safe.
